datasets.name: TransformFit Internal Funnel Event Logs
datasets.source: Supabase database — Michael exports raw session/event data; Pulse defines 7-event schema to instrument before redesign ships
datasets.size_estimate: < 10MB at current user volume; schema designed to scale to 1GB at 10K users

datasets.name: LLM API Call Logs with Token Counts and Cost-Per-Interaction
datasets.source: OpenAI or Anthropic usage dashboard export; Michael pulls historical API logs with model, token input/output counts, and timestamp

datasets.name: Current Onboarding Flow Step Map and Form Field Inventory
datasets.source: Internal asset review — Michael documents each onboarding screen, field labels, input types, and current time-to-first-workout from React frontend codebase

datasets.name: Fitbod and Noom App Store Review Corpus
datasets.source: Apple App Store and Google Play scrape via app-scraper or SerpAPI filtered for onboarding, coaching continuity, memory, abandonment, and motivation mentions — minimum 500 reviews per app, current quarter
datasets.format: Chunked text embeddings in Supabase pgvector
datasets.size_estimate: ~5MB raw text; ~50MB embedded vectors at 1536 dimensions
datasets.cost: $10–$20 one-time SerpAPI or apify.com scrape cost

datasets.name: Behavioral Economics Digital Health Retention Evidence Base
datasets.source: PubMed, Google Scholar, and ACM Digital Library — curated corpus of 30–50 peer-reviewed studies on commitment devices, loss aversion, variable reward schedules, and implementation intentions in digital health and fitness apps for adults 25–45
datasets.format: PDF ingestion → chunked text embeddings in Supabase pgvector
datasets.cost: free — open-access papers; $0 embedding cost via Supabase pgvector on free tier

datasets.name: LLM Provider Pricing and Model Tier Reference Sheet
datasets.source: OpenAI pricing page, Anthropic pricing page, and Together.ai / Groq open-source tier docs — manually compiled by Ledger and Nova at sprint start; refreshed monthly
datasets.format: JSON lookup table keyed by model ID with input/output cost per 1M tokens, context window, and caching availability flags

datasets.name: Existing Supabase User Profile Schema and Sample Records
datasets.source: Michael exports current Supabase table definitions (pg_dump schema-only) and 10–20 anonymized user profile rows to surface what is currently stored per user
datasets.format: SQL schema DDL + JSON sample records

datasets.name: Fitness App Onboarding Teardown Case Library
datasets.source: UX Collective, Mobbin.com, and Product Hunt teardown articles — curated set of 15–25 documented fitness and health app onboarding flows annotated with aha-moment timing, field counts, and conversion outcomes; supplemented by App Experience Studio's internal pattern library
datasets.format: Chunked markdown/HTML embeddings in Supabase pgvector
datasets.cost: $0 — Mobbin free tier sufficient for MVP; all sources publicly accessible

datasets.name: Reddit and Community Forum Fitness App Sentiment Corpus
datasets.source: Reddit API (r/Fitness, r/loseit, r/Biohackers, r/apps) and relevant fitness subreddits — threads mentioning Fitbod, Noom, AI coaching, onboarding frustration, and app abandonment; filtered to last 6 months
datasets.format: Chunked text embeddings in Supabase pgvector
datasets.cost: $0 — Reddit API free tier at current pull volume; ~100 API calls

datasets.name: AI Agent Persistent Memory Architecture Reference Corpus
datasets.source: LangChain documentation, MemGPT paper, OpenAI Cookbook, Anthropic prompt engineering guide, and 5–10 curated blog posts on hybrid structured-profile plus rolling-summarization memory patterns — assembled by Nova and Knowledge Engineer
datasets.format: Chunked markdown/PDF embeddings in Supabase pgvector

datasets.name: Workout Generation Prompt Templates and Token Consumption Baseline
datasets.source: Michael exports current FastAPI prompt templates used for workout generation — raw prompt strings with placeholder variables, plus 10–20 sampled actual API request/response pairs showing real token counts

datasets.name: Day-1 / Day-7 / Day-30 Cohort Retention Baseline Extract
datasets.source: Supabase — Pulse defines and backfills cohort retention query against existing user registration timestamps and any available session timestamps; generates pre-redesign baseline even if event coverage is sparse

external_apis.name: Apify App Store Review Scraper
external_apis.purpose: Automated bulk scrape of Fitbod, Noom, and MyFitnessPal App Store and Google Play reviews filtered by keyword and date range — feeds the competitive review corpus used by app_experience_studio, freya, and compass for gap analysis
external_apis.cost: $10–$20 one-time run on Apify free/starter tier; ~2,000 reviews per app

external_apis.name: Reddit API (Free Tier)
external_apis.purpose: Pull community forum threads mentioning Fitbod, AI fitness coaching, onboarding friction, and app abandonment from fitness and wellness subreddits — feeds Freya and Mira's persona language pack and sentiment corpus
external_apis.cost: Free — standard Reddit API access at < 100 requests per minute well within free tier

external_apis.name: OpenAI Embeddings API (text-embedding-3-small)
external_apis.purpose: Generate vector embeddings for all RAG corpora (review corpus, behavioral economics papers, teardown case library, memory architecture docs) stored in Supabase pgvector — lowest cost embedding model at $0.02 per 1M tokens
external_apis.cost: ~$2–$5 one-time embedding cost for full corpus build across all 12 datasets; negligible ongoing cost for incremental updates

external_apis.name: Supabase REST API and pgvector Extension
external_apis.purpose: Primary vector store and structured data layer for all agent RAG retrieval, user profile persistence, session memory, event tracking schema, and row-level security enforcement — already in tech stack
external_apis.cost: Free tier sufficient for MVP; $25/month Pro tier required if storage exceeds 500MB or if function invocations exceed free limits at 500+ active users

external_apis.name: PubMed E-utilities API
external_apis.purpose: Programmatic retrieval of open-access behavioral economics and digital health retention research papers — feeds Freya's evidence base for retention mechanic ranking with effect sizes
external_apis.cost: Free — NIH public API with no cost at research-volume query rates

total_estimated_cost: $22–$45 one-time data acquisition; $0–$25/month ongoing depending on Supabase tier trigger at user scale
